_template: world-seed v1.1 — fill this in before initializing a new root. See ADR-029.
_usage: Copy to runs/{rootId}/world-seed.json when creating a new root. Remove all _guide and _template fields before committing the live version.
_supersedes: world v1.0 (runs/root_2026_04_14/world-seed.json) — v1.0 was declared retroactively after the April 2026 root ran without a formal seed. v1.1 is the first version authored before a root begins.

geography._guide: Name the places that will persist across all days of this root. Be specific but small — 4-6 named places is enough. Places that appear unnamed in stories become harder to reference consistently. Each place accumulates meaning over the month; start only with what you know will actually appear.

home_base._guide: Where YY starts and ends each day. The sleep location, the morning departure point.

named_places._guide: One entry per named place. Copy this object for each additional place.
named_places.type: _guide: cache_site | path | boundary | social_node | shelter | unknown

geography.territory_notes: _guide: Rough description of YY's range this month. What's familiar, what's at the edge, what's outside.

seasonal_physics._guide: What does this month mean physically for a squirrel? These aren't plot elements — they're the background physics that every day operates against. The pipeline reads these to understand what 'normal' looks like before any event occurs.
seasonal_physics.month_character: _guide: One phrase. E.g. 'late spring recovery', 'peak summer heat', 'pre-winter cache-building'.

food_pressure._guide: Float 0-1. 0 = scarcity/crisis, 0.5 = neutral, 1 = abundance. This is the ambient food availability before YY does anything. Spring post-winter is typically 0.4-0.6 (recovering). Fall pre-winter is 0.7-0.9 (building).

seasonal_physics.territorial_pressure: _guide: How contested is space this month? Spring has more transient animals moving through. Summer has established territories. Describe the social density.
seasonal_physics.daylight: _guide: What's the light like? Length of day, quality. Long spring days vs. short winter ones change YY's available window.
seasonal_physics.conditions: _guide: Weather texture for the month — not specific forecasts, the general physical feel. Warmth, rain patterns, wind.
seasonal_physics.morning_cache_window: _guide: When is it open, how reliable is it, what affects it this month? This is the highest-stakes recurring event in a squirrel's day.

social_field._guide: Who or what is in the territory? Named presences don't need backstory — they need enough description to recur consistently. 'The grey squirrel from the east boundary' is enough to be recognizable when it shows up again. Keep it small: 2-3 named presences maximum at root start. They can acquire more identity over the month.
social_field.density: _guide: Low | medium | high. How many other animals are actively present in YY's range right now?

known_presences._guide: Copy for each recurring presence. These are not characters — they are consistent environmental elements with agency.

active_tensions: _guide: Anything already in play at root start. A contested cache site. A boundary dispute. An unknown animal that appeared last month. These carry into the first days automatically.

starting_conditions._guide: YY's physical state at the beginning of this root. These become state_before for Day 1's snapshot. They should reflect seasonal_physics honestly — a squirrel starting a root in March post-winter has lower food than one starting in October.
identity_notes: _guide: 1-2 notes about where YY is psychologically at root start. What's carried forward from the previous root, if anything. What the clean-slate version of YY looks like.

atmosphere._guide: The texture of this month as a narrative resource. Not what will happen — what's available. What recurring elements will show up. What this month costs and what it offers. The pipeline uses this to find appropriate event translations — a month with 'heat and slowing down' translates world events differently than one with 'urgency and preparation'.
atmosphere.one_line: _guide: A single phrase that captures the feel of this month in YY's world. E.g. 'the long work before abundance', 'the quiet that comes after', 'everything moving too fast'.
recurring_elements: _guide: Environmental things that will keep showing up. Sounds, light qualities, seasonal activity patterns. E.g. 'longer evenings', 'other animals competing for the same routes', 'rain that arrives without warning'.
atmosphere.what_this_month_costs: _guide: What pressure does YY operate under? What does the month take?
atmosphere.what_this_month_offers: _guide: What's available that other months don't have? What can YY find or experience that's specific to this period?

authorial_intent._guide: What do you, the author, want to track across this month? Not plot — direction. E.g. 'I want to see what happens when the cache_deficit resolves or compounds over 30 days.' Or: 'I want to explore what curiosity looks like under sustained resource pressure.' Optional but useful for multi-week arcs.
authorial_intent.branch_hypothesis: _guide: If you already know what divergence you want to explore, name it here. E.g. 'what happens if one branch encounters a cooperative presence vs. a competitive one.'
